text: Laparoscopic spleen preserving distal pancreatectomy. A thirty-six-year-old woman, presenting with epigastric fullness, abdominal CT revealed a multiloculated cyst at the tail of the pancreas.

The surgical team uses five laparoscopic ports, including two 12mm and three 5mm ports. The gastrocolic ligament is incised with a harmonic scalpel, revealing the tumor located at the tail of the pancreas. An incision is made along the lower border of the pancreas.

The surgeon reflects the pancreas upward to expose the splenic vein at its upper border. Using a harmonic scalpel, the splenic vein is carefully dissected a few millimeters away from the pancreatic vein into the pancreatic parenchyma, ensuring complete hemostasis and preventing thermal injury to the vein.

The surgeon controls the splenic vein and artery with vessel loops. The pancreas is divided proximal to the tumor using a linear stapler with a triple staggered three-row configuration. The remaining pancreatic tissue is dissected away from the splenic vessels, concluding the procedure.
The histologic examination of the specimen revealed a serous cystadenoma of the pancreas. Thank you very much for your kind attention. Any questions will be more than welcome.
